programme.desc: Historical epic spectacularly re-enacting the heroic efforts of a handful of Spartan soldiers against the full might of the Persian army at Thermopylae in 480 BC.

programme.title: Jerry & Marge Go Large
programme.desc: Jerry Selbee discovers a mathematical loophole in the Massachusetts lottery, and with the help of his wife, Marge, he wins millions of dollars.

programme.title: Entrapment
programme.desc: An unlikely partnership is formed by an insurance investigator and an ageing cat burglar. When a heavily guarded work of art is stolen, the insurance investigator persuades her employers to let her go undercover and team up with a master thief. The thief sets the woman a rigorous training programme before they embark on a series of robberies against an emotional backdrop of distrust and growing attraction.

programme.title: Angel Has Fallen
programme.desc: Wrongfully accused of trying to kill the president, a framed Secret Service agent must evade the FBI and his own agency to uncover the truth and save the country from imminent danger.

programme.desc: Fieri's diving into texture and flavour! A BBQ bistro in Sherman Oaks, CA, is cooking up the-bomb brisket noodles. A Cincinnati diner is plating top-notch chili crunch chicken nuggets. A funky spot in Austin, TX is serving up a primo pasta margarita.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's in Sicily, Italy, digging into the local specialities! A cliffside spot is serving primo prawns over squid ink pasta, a mouthwatering menu is offering up A-list arancini, and a family-run joint is scratch-making next-level ravioli.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy checks out breakfast from the East to the Southwest.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy explores local favourites with hot meat sandwiches in San Francisco, a fish market in Omaha, and an Italian spot in Pittsburgh.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richman's eating adventure has brought him to beautiful Mobile, Alabama. First, Adam heads over to the Hungry Owl to try a burger that Mobile natives say is the 'best in 'Bama'.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richman's tasty travels have brought him to the Florida Keys, an archipelago at the southernmost tip of the continental United States. The Florida Keys is the fishing capital of the world.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: A sampling of Wisconsin's finest meats and an eating challenge in under 23 minutes.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: The nationwide quest for tasty eats has led Adam Richman to the historic city of Savannah, Georgia. First, Adam heads to Sweet Potatoes for a taste of peach glazed BBQ chicken, a southern classic.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam's cross-country culinary quest brings him to Portsmouth, New Hampshire. Adam's first stop is Gilley's, which has been serving up delicious burgers for nearly a century.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam's culinary crusade takes him on a road trip along the beautiful Gulf Coast in search of delicious eats. Adam begins his journey at The Shed in Ocean Springs, Mississippi.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam's all-American eats lands him in Louisville, Kentucky, home to bluegrass, bourbon and the Kentucky Derby. Adam starts off at the legendary Brown Hotel to try a taste of the original Hot Brown.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam travels to Dallas to sample Texas-style bratwurst and BBQ brisket; a Texan seeks victory over five pounds of Vietnamese Pho in under 30 minutes.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richman's eating expedition has brought him to the capital city of the Ocean State - Providence, Rhode Island. Adam first heads over to Bob And Timmy's to try their unique grilled pizza.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richman's tasty travels take him on a road trip across the Main Street of America, Route 66. Adam's first stop is in St Louis, Missouri, to try some famous frozen custards.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James prepares roasted garlic bread, salmon with garlic and champ and pan-fried dauphinoise potatoes. To see how garlic grows in the UK, James takes a trip to the Isle of Wight.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James applies his know-how to barbecued spare ribs, pork terrine and roast belly pork. He also visits Andover farmers' market to find the best local ingredients.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James prepares olive oil focaccia with rosemary, anchovy pizza and poppy seed snap visits De Gustibus bakery in London to get some tips from a master baker.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James Martin prepares red onion and creme fraiche pizza, watercress and scallop salad and watercress and Stilton soup. The country around Winchester is famous for watercress, and James visits a grower and learns that the secret is in the water.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Ten passionate homebakers take part in a `bake off', which will test every aspect of their baking skills as they battle it out to be crowned the Great British Bake Off's Best Amateur Baker. Each week the bakers are put through three challenges. The competition kicks off with cake in the Cotswolds. The bakers tackle three increasingly difficult challenges, starting off with their signature bake.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins take the eight passionate home bakers who made it through the first round to Scone Palace near Perth to make biscuits and teatime treats.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It is week three of the competition and the six remaining bakers are making bread in Kent. In the shadow of Sarre Windmill, the bakers will be kneading, proving and knocking back their dough under the watchful eye of baking writer Mary Berry and master baker Paul Hollywood.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's week four and the remaining five bakers have travelled to Bakewell in Derbyshire. This time the bakers are reinventing an often neglected British classic - the pudding.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's the penultimate round and as the travelling marquee pitches up in the Cornish village of Mousehole, it's time for the bakers to get to grips with the most difficult of all baking skills - pastry. They bake their own versions of hearty British pies, get down to details with exquisite pastry canapes, and take a crash-course in crimping for this week's surprise bake.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's the Grand Final and the last three surviving contestants face their biggest challenge yet - baking for the Bake Off's Afternoon Tea Party. In order to be crowned the victor, they will need to bring together all of their skills, making cakes, bread and pastry.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Football
programme.desc: Adam Richman combines his two great passions - food and football. He visits Britain's most famous teams and seeks out footie's finest foods!

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Football
programme.desc: Adam Richman combines his two great passions, food and football. He visits Britain's most famous teams and seeks out footie's finest foods.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam heads to Portland, Oregon where a habanero fritter challenge await him.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam tackles a five pound omelette made with 12 eggs.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James' British adventure has crossed over the Irish sea to Northern Ireland, where he is joined by his great pal and famous local chef, Paul Rankin. Their first port of call is the Giant's Causeway, where James settles right in and cooks up a fish stew for the crowds of tourists. Paul goes in search of Irish gold and finds it in the form of rapeseed at the Kane family farm in Limavady.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Paul are having a blast in Northern Ireland and they've reached Paul's home town of Belfast, where he's giving James a personal tour of Northern Ireland's capital city before they head off for a spot of golf. James pitches up his mobile kitchen right on the green to whip up a tasty dish with bacon, scallops and apples with onion rings. After all his exertion on the golf course, Paul heads just out of town to Carnbrooke Meats.

programme.title: Roux Down The River
programme.desc: In Southend, Michel meets a family of fishmongers and chefs keeping it local. Plus, he makes scallop curry, prawn toast, and fried custard beignets.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy tries fine fast food with green chilli cheeseburgers in New Mexico, deep fried tacos in Omaha and hot dogs in Connecticut.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy samples some funky joints, there's a gas station serving duck l'orange and a cafe where circus performers serve pasta with elk.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy tries beef stew made from tenderloin in Arizona and in Chicago he visits a bar with burgers named after rock bands.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy tastes international favourites including French pate in Sacramento, Basque specialties in Idaho and Latin flavours in Florida.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam's culinary crusade takes him on a road trip along the beautiful Pacific Coast Highway in search of some deliciously tasty eats. Adam begins his journey at The Reel Inn in Malibu.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richman's culinary quest has him hitting the streets to seek out the best eats. First, he heads south to Tampa Bay, where he samples some authentic Mexican tacos.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: The team behind the St. Piggy's Day ask Buddy and his team to make a cake fit for a pig. That means real bacon butter-cream.

programme.title: Cake Boss

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaney's BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castel's aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwell's taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaney's BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castel's aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwell's taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs who've made it through cook their four courses again. They're hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined this week by tennis' greatest entertainer, Mansour Bahrami.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With Finals Week ahead of them, judge Andi Oliver first summons the chefs to Wimbledon to take a look at where they could be cooking if they win. The judges are joined by guest judge Sue Barker, host of the Wimbledon coverage for more than 20 years.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but with some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the regional heats, each chef will need to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. The judges are joined by guest judge Tim Henman.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. As the barbecues are fired up, all 8 chefs are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by guest judgeand Paralympic gold medallist, Gordon Reid.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is dessert day and with just one place remaining at the Wimbledon banquet, each chef is giving everything they have got. The judges are joined by guest judge Annabel Croft, former British Number One and Junior Wimbledon Champion.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the grand finale and the winning chefs must now cook their dishes at the banquet. Once in the kitchen, the chefs face the huge challenge of scaling up their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for top spot are Danny Parker, head chef at Michelin starred `House of Tides' in Newcastle, who is back to try his luck in the competition again after being knocked out in the regional heat last year following a tense tie-break. He's taking on two newcomers: Dan Fletcher, protégé of Phil Howard, and now head chef at `Fenchurch' in the City of London, and David Coulson,who runs `Peace and Loaf' in Newcastle, which boasts 2 AA rosettes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course the chefs are all aiming for 10s with their unique fish dishes. David is celebrating a personal connection to the brief with his `My Cod I Bet Me Ma'am Has Seen Some Trotters', a tribute to his mum who has worked as a chiropodist. Dan's `For All Those Sleepless Nights' pays tribute to nightshift workers from the NHS with some bold but classic flavours while Danny's `Not All Superheroes Wear Capes' aims to give a taste of luxury to the hardworking staff.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dan pays tribute to an icon of modern nursing, Florence Nightingale, while Danny in inspired by the founder of the NHS, Aneurin Bevan. David's dish is dedicated to the air ambulance staff who saved the life of his fiancé, and he even takes a trip to meet one of them.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the chefs' last chance to impress and tensions are high. Dan takes a risk with an unusual combination of ewe's milk and bergamot while Danny is determined to pull out all the stops with his complex dessert `Aunty Gwen's Chocolate'. David hopes to transform a childhood comfort classic into a show stopping dessert. Dan also takes a trip to meet Dr Ben Clarke who was working in the Royal London Hospital A&E during the London Bridge terror attack.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again, but will they take on board their veteran's advice as they serve their `Feast To Say Thank You'?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Banquet champion Selin Kiazim, a rising star of the London scene renowned for her Turkish Cypriot fusion flavours, is back hoping to join the elite group of chefs who have made it to the banquet twice. She is up against James Cochran who's worked at some of London's toughest kitchens including 2 Michelin starred `The Ledbury' and Scott Goss from `The Twenty Six' in Tunbridge Wells, renowned for his use of local Kent ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs do battle for the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen is tense. Lobster is catch of the day for James whose dish is Caribbean-inspired. Scott is attempting to elevate a classic fish pie, dedicated to his mum who worked as a pharmacist in the NHS for 23 years. And Selin is taking a risk with a complex three-part fish dish featuring sardines, scallops and langoustines, as a nod to the purported health benefits of a Mediterranean diet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scott is showcasing rare breed Tamworth pork to say thank you to the rare breed of people who work for the NHS. Selin is serving a complex duck dish using ingredients from all around the world to honour `Our Global NHS'. And James is presenting five cuts of goat cooked in five different ways, for his bold dish `Under The Knife'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Selin puts her own unique twist on tea and toast. James creates a decadent coconut parfait accompanied by mango puree filled doughnuts for his dessert `Tree of Life' inspired by the coconut palm tree, the NHS and both of their contributions to our health. Scott hopes his elevated gypsy tart `Honey Bee Good' will secure him a place in the final.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their four-course menus again, but will they take on board their veteran's advice as they re-make their `Feast To Say Thank You'?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Vying to be crowned champion of Scotland are three talented newcomers to the GBM kitchen. Lorna McNee is a former Scottish Chef of the Year and is renowned for her technical precision which she has gained from working her entire career at 2 Michelin-starred restaurant Andrew Fairlie at Gleneagles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and the chefs are all out to impress. The heat is increased as all three serve their unique takes on lobster. Ross is hoping to snatch victory with his dish `Lobster Aid'. Lorna's `Live Well, Live Very Well' features only three elements, each of which she needs to deliver perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ross is hoping to impress with `Oh Deer It's Time For Your Penicillin', his tribute to Scottish scientist Alexander Fleming featuring an unusual yeast element as well as venison. Across the kitchen, Lorna, a former Game Chef of the Year, is also cooking venison in a bid to impress.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's dessert day and the last chance for the chefs to impress before one is sent home. Ross is attempting to elevate a comfort classic into a dish worthy of the banquet. Lorna's cooking a personal tribute to the NHS staff who have cared for her during treatment for a rare blood condition, using unusual yuzu and tonka bean flavours. Once again Ben is serving an experimental dish with his `Dance for the Greater Good' which includes a bees wax ice cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's judging day and the two remaining chefs must impress the panel of Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and Matthew Fort. Both chefs are gunning for success and are doing everything they can to deliver their menus to perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs compete in the heat for the central region. Returner Ryan Simson-Trotman, who failed to reach the national finals last year, is originally from Nuneaton and opened his own restaurant Orwells in the Oxfordshire countryside with his partner Liam, who is also a contestant in the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and the chefs are pulling out all the stops. Marianne pays tribute to the `angels' in her local hospital who looked after her during a recent illness. Ryan's dish is a reference to the purported health benefits of fish high in omega 3 oils. While Sabrina is inspired by her aunt, a retired nurse who used to take in food to share with her fellow workers during her shifts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and following yesterday's results, tension is high. Both Marianne and Ryan are cooking muntjac in the hope of impressing the veteran judge, while Sabrina is making an ambitious technical take on a family feast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With one dish left to impress the veteran judge, the chefs cook their desserts. Marianne is looking to raise a smile with her `carry on' themed dessert, complete with surprise presentation. Sabrina hopes to make it through with an elevated take on jelly and ice cream while Ryan goes all out with his very sweet treat `A Spoonful of Sugar'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining central region chefs do battle for a place in the national finals. They must impress the formidable panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton with their four course menus.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andrew is attempting to pay tribute to NHS staff with his innovative take on a traditional healing recipe, `Mum's Flu Fighting Chicken Soup'. Jason's `A Tea From The Heart' is a play on a post-war remedy which harks back to the era when the NHS was formed. And Chris is hoping to impress with his dish inspired by a therapeutic hospital garden featuring foraged herbs from the Welsh valley where he lives including wild chervil, pickled pine buds and yarrow tips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After the starter results, the chefs return to the kitchen hoping for high scores with their fish dishes. Chris once again is using ingredients he has foraged from around his restaurant, while Jason hopes to impress with a spicy scallop dish and Andrew prepares a cod dish with 11 different elements.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are pulling out all the stops in a bid for big scores with their mains. All three dishes pay tribute to the founder of the NHS Aneurin Bevan, Chris's `Everything But The Squeal' is an ambitious pork sharing platter, while Jason's `For The Rich And The Poor' showcases lamb, and Andrew is hoping to impress with `Aneurin Bevan For Better Steak'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and the last opportunity for the chefs to impress. Chris is hoping to triumph with another dish which showcases the foraged ingredients available around his restaurant, while Jason attempts a technical feat in his dessert inspired by a childhood hospital stay and Andrew is inspired by a dish by his great grandmother used to cook. Which two will make it through to the judging chamber?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Wales regional final as the two remaining chefs do everything they can to impress the judging panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. After tensions ran high in the week, the chefs are under pressure to deliver perfection. The judges are joined by Aneira Thomas, the first baby born on the NHS, who is named in tribute to the service's founder Aneurin Bevan and who went on to dedicate her life to the service. Who will win a place in the national finals?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the turn of three chefs representing the South West, including two who have competed before but meet in the kitchen this week for the very first time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and with Tom renowned as a fish specialist all eyes are on him. He's cooking his take on a classic British fish supper in tribute to a charity which looks after the health of local fishermen. Jude is attempting a complex fusion dish featuring four different fish and an unusual X Ray themed presentation, while Olivia is paying tribute to the staff who looked after her during the birth of her first son with her take on curried monkfish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the main course and two of the chefs are hoping duck will win the day. Tom is preparing a technically challenging duck dish steeped in NHS history while Jude is making a multi-element Chinese duck dish inspired by his mum. Olivia is hoping her dish, using two unusual cuts of beef, will score top points. Olivia also visits her local children's hospital, home of the second busiest paediatric A&E in the country.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With just one more chance to impress their veteran judge, the chefs are all hoping to use their pastry skills to deliver technically complex desserts. Tom's cooking an `Invalid Fruit Tart' inspired by a 1950s recipe, while Jude is making six different apple elements for his `An Apple A Day'. Olivia is using stout in reference to an outdated old wives' tale which suggested the drink was a healthy source of iron during pregnancy.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's time for the two remaining chefs representing the South West to go head to head as they cook their menus again for the exacting panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. With a spot in the National Finals at stake, both are aiming for perfection as they bid for glory. The judges are joined by Barbara Childs, a Matron in the Cardiac Intensive Care Unit at Great Ormond Street Hospital, who isn't afraid to speak her mind in the chamber.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs take to the kitchen in a bid to represent the North West in the national final. They include Ellis Barrie, from the award winning Marram Grass in Anglesey, Liam Simpson-Trotman, an accomplished pastry chef and co-owner of the 4 AA rosette Orwell's in Oxfordshire, and Craig Sherrington, who sharpened his skills working under top names including Eric Chavot and is now Chef Patron of Virginia House in Cumbria.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs take to the kitchen in a bid to represent the North West in the national final. They include Ellis Barrie, from the award winning Marram Grass in Anglesey, Liam Simpson-Trotman, an accomplished pastry chef and co-owner of the 4 AA rosette Orwell's in Oxfordshire, and Craig Sherrington, who sharpened his skills working under top names including Eric Chavot and is now Chef Patron of Virginia House in Cumbria.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and the chefs are all gunning for tens. Craig pays tribute to a friend who has revolutionised hospital food in Morecambe Bay, while Liam is inspired by health advice his grandma gave him and Ellis is dedicating his dish to the staff who delivered his son Albert. With cod, mackerel, shrimp and cockles on the menu, which of the chefs will score highly?

programme.desc: Lise Meitner and Otto Hahn were the dream team of nuclear physics in the 1930s. For the convinced pacifist Meitner, it was unthinkable to put her work at the service of a weapon of mass destruction produced by the USA.

programme.title: The Real Oppenheimer
programme.desc: In 1945, the world was ushered into the atomic age. With his role as the Father of the Bomb solidified, Oppenheimer became increasingly worried about the potential danger that scientific inventions could pose to humanity.

programme.title: Victory Remembered, Legacy of The Black Devils

programme.title: Battles Won & Lost
programme.desc: New tactics are born in every war, to try to influence the course of battles. Campaigns are waged against non-military targets to ruin morale; long battles rage throughout the war, from Guadalcanal in the Pacific, to the brutal siege at Leningrad.

programme.title: Battles Won & Lost
programme.desc: World war involves many actions, large and small. Forces, climate, geography, and problems of supply must all be managed; Japan gathers naval strength for an attack on Midway Island as they attempt to lure the United States into a trap.

programme.title: Battles Won & Lost
programme.desc: In every battle lies victory and defeat, decided by strategy and which side is better able to manage resources. The battles won and lost determine the outcome of World War II.

programme.title: Battles Won & Lost

programme.title: In Defence Of The Reich: Hitler'S Atlantic Wall

programme.title: Battle for North America
programme.desc: Telling the story of the Battle of Quebec, 1759, where at stake was the future of North America and the fate of the British Empire. Britain used its growing industrial strength and a new scientific approach to fight a campaign unlike any that had gone before. It launched a fleet of 200 ships carrying 20,000 men on a deadly mission through uncharted waters.

programme.title: Civil War: The Untold Story
programme.desc: On April 6, 1862, the Confederate force's surprise attack against the Union in Shiloh, Tenn., results in some of the most brutal fighting of the Civil War.

programme.title: Civil War: The Untold Story
programme.desc: Gen. Ulysses S. Grant's counter-attack at Shiloh, Tenn., leads to victory; thousands of escaping slaves seek refuge with Union forces advancing into the South; President Abraham Lincoln begins drafting a proclamation addressing slavery and freedom.

programme.title: Civil War: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The Emancipation Proclamation ends slavery and gives blacks the freedom to fight; on July 4, 1863, the day after Pickett's Charge at Gettysburg, the Confederates surrender Vicksburg, Miss., to Gen. Grant.

programme.title: Civil War: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The Confederate Army of Northern Virginia succeeds at splitting the Union Army in two during the Battle of Chickamauga; Gen. William Rosecrans is replaced by Gen. Ulysses S. Grant, who plans an offensive.

programme.title: Civil War: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The Confederate Army falls to a defensive position in Atlanta; President Abraham Lincoln defeats challenger George McClellan; Sherman's March to the Sea.

programme.title: The Origins of Warfare
programme.desc: Hiroo Onoda's story reflects warfare's cultural significance, freedom, and honor struggles.

programme.title: Battles Won & Lost
programme.desc: New tactics are born in every war, to try to influence the course of battles. Campaigns are waged against non-military targets to ruin morale; long battles rage throughout the war, from Guadalcanal in the Pacific, to the brutal siege at Leningrad.

programme.title: Battles Won & Lost
programme.desc: World war involves many actions, large and small. Forces, climate, geography, and problems of supply must all be managed; Japan gathers naval strength for an attack on Midway Island as they attempt to lure the United States into a trap.

programme.title: Battles Won & Lost
programme.desc: In every battle lies victory and defeat, decided by strategy and which side is better able to manage resources. The battles won and lost determine the outcome of World War II.

programme.title: Battles Won & Lost
programme.desc: In every battle lies victory and defeat, decided by strategy and which side is better able to manage resources. The battles won and lost determine the outcome of World War II.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: Myths and legends affect the consolidation of the rules of society, as well as the punishments that result from the violation of the rules.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: Society has always has had a fascination with the dead and the myths and legends associated with the afterlife reflect what different cultures value most in life.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: Discovering the tragic Greek myth of Actaeon, who stumbled upon the goddess Artemis, along with the Celtic Otherworld, an idyllic place of magic and beauty, and hunting for the Kraken before venturing into the woods of the Brothers Grimm.

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Obsessions
programme.desc: Liza Tarback on Weather: Liza Tarbuck tackles the national obsession with all things weather-related. Season finale.

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Obsessions
programme.desc: A host of top celebrities explore uniquely British preoccupations and passions in an attempt to find out what makes the British tick.

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Obsessions
programme.desc: Suggs discovers the history of the British love of the humble hostelry and explores a load of interesting issues along the way, from women's role in brewing, and the community role of the pub, to the pub as a place of literary and musical creativity.

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Obsessions
programme.desc: Chris Packham sets out to discover when animals first became our "best friends", and which kind of pets Brits prefer. During his investigation he meets a pet psychic and a real-life pet detective, he then visits a rescue centre for exotic pets.

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Obsessions

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s bungalow in Dereham in Norfolk, a first-floor flat in London's East Ham and a property in Maryport in Cumbria with a hidden room are all sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear their plans for their investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a semi in Ilkeston in Derby, a flat in Streatham Hill in London and a bungalow in Somerset's Portishead.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a fifth-floor flat in Greater Manchester, a three bed semi in Carlisle and three commercial buildings near Stanstead.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An old Derbyshire farmhouse in Wirksworth gets a stunning make over, a developer builds a pair of semis in Chatham in Kent while a mid-terraced house in Blackhall Colliery in County Durham is given a makeover ready for the rental market.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion meet father and daughter developers. An apartment in a former mill in Mow Cop on the Staffordshire /Cheshire borders, a three-bed semi in East Ham and a flat in Bournemouth are all given makeovers with the help of Dad.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple about to get married buy their first home in Newcastle, a new dad thinks developing a flat in Leyton in London will give him more time with his four-month-old son, and a former IT worker develops a property in Gainsborough in Lincolnshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat over two floors in London's Camberwell, another flat in Telford in Shropshire and a three-bed semi in Alvaston in Derby are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a grand five-bedroom period house in Folkestone in Kent, a two bedroom flat in London's Leytonstone and a two bedroom mid terrace in Fenton in Stoke on Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A studio flat in a former 1960's office block which is grade two listed in London's Elephant and Castle, a terraced house in Pontygwaith in South Wales and a flat in in Cradley Heath near Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with no floor in Edgbaston in Birmingham, a detached house on several levels in Oldham and a former garage complex ripe for development in Forest Hill in London are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A converted 16th century chapel in Matlock in Derbyshire, a two bedroom flat in Whetstone in London and a water damaged house in Wishaw in Scotland are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two bed semi in Meir in Stoke on Trent is given a country house look, a flat in London's Willesden Green also gets the wow factor and a semi in Dudley in the West Midlands loses it's 1980's look to be brought into the 21st century.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion meet property developers who have all bought at auction and hope to make a profit on their investments. Today a landscape gardener tackles a terrace in Ilkeston in Derby, two friends take on a four storey house in Sittingbourne in Kent while another couple of friends' first venture together is a studio flat in South Norwood in London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Terraced houses in Gravesend in Kent and Shipley in Derbyshire are given a new lease of life while former church premises in Harrow Weald in Middlesex are given a new use for the community.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The trio look at a family home in Kent, a former bar in Llanelli in Wales and a terrace in Croydon, all sold at auction. Will any of the developers make a return on their investments?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom semi in Crewe, a top-floor flat in London and a terraced house in Oldham are sold under the hammer. All the owners are happy with the prices they paid, but will any of them be able to turn a profit at the end of the projects?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: While developers in Croydon in Surrey and Donnington in Shropshire wrestle with the question of whether to lose a bedroom each for a better placed kitchen or bathroom, two friends in Derby consider creating two more in their six-bedroom house.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a rundown cottage in Probus in Cornwall, a father buys a flat in Mitcham in Surrey as a long-term investment for his young daughter and a seasoned property developer buys a house in Tunstall in Stoke-on-Trent for her daughter.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: In Florida, a boat thief runs from the law, kicking off an adrenaline-fueled speedboat chase. A night-time patrol shifts up a gear when a six-ton U-Hual suspected of harbouring stolen goods is spotted.

programme.desc: Terrifying true stories of mothers and their children when nature's strongest bond morphs into conspiracy, manipulation and murder. Acts of greed, revenge and cruelty leave trails of torment and destruction across America.

programme.title: A Body in the Basement
programme.desc: Philadelphia police officers find four adults locked in a filthy basement dungeon as the investigation leads detectives across four states, resulting in multiple felony arrests; all evidence points to a single ringleader behind a deadly scheme.

programme.title: Signs of a Psychopath
programme.desc: A young woman makes a frantic 911 call about a brutal home invasion that results in the shooting of her mother and father. As police interview her, they uncover a secret double life and an elaborate web of lies spun by a maniacal psychopath.

programme.title: Signs of a Psychopath
programme.desc: A lone gunman shoots a young man and woman on their way home from the gym where they work. While interrogating a suspect, detectives learn that this is no random attack but the twisted plan of a deranged psychopathic stalker.

programme.title: Signs of a Psychopath

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: Katherine Kelly examines the 1986 murder of 16-year-old Elaine Doyle. One of Scotland's longest-running murder enquiries, it took 26 years to find her killer.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: With first-hand testimony from family members, police officers, witnesses and reporters, we shine a light on some of Britain's darkest murder locations, examining the crimes that rocked these communities.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: This series looks at some of Britain's darkest murder locations. With Anita Rani at the helm this series promises to shine new light on some of the most fascinating cases from all.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: When a woman is brutally attacked while walking her dog in 1995, a local man evades justice once, but not twice, thanks to the double jeopardy law change.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: The story of the murder of 72-year-old Jane Hings. 25-year-old Craig Keogh comes under suspicion when he is seen acting aggressively on the night of the murder.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: Katherine Kelly explores the tragic murder of 15-year-old Naomi Smith, and how a series of breakthroughs and DNA evidence led police to her killer.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: Katherine Kelly looks into the 2011 murder of Adam Vincent, a killing so brutal it sparked North Lincolnshire's biggest police investigation in over a century.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: Katherine Kelly explores the tragic death of Cassandra McDermott. It took a brutal attack on another woman and a decade of chasing before her killer was caught.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: This series looks at some of Britain's darkest murder locations. With Anita Rani at the helm this series promises to shine new light on some of the most fascinating cases from all.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: The story of Pennie Davis, whose feud with her ex-partner's son leads him to take extreme measures that she paid for with her life.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: A gruesome mystery unfolds when the remains of Wendy Upton are discovered in a Lichfield woods, and a man helping police with the investigation is arrested.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: When a young woman goes missing on Valentine's Day, detectives turn to a pollen expert, who pieces together fragments of evidence to find her body.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: In 1993, 16-year-old Claire Tiltman is found dying by the roadside. Her killer disappears into the night. More than 20 years later, Colin Ash Smith is finally brought to trial. Jurors hear of how he knew Claire.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: In April 2012, Dawn and George Kibble visited the flat of Dawn's sister Julie Davison, concerned for her well-being. They found Julie's body on the blood-stained floor of her kitchen. She'd been attacked, stabbed multiple times, and left for dead.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: `Happy Valley' actress Katherine Kelly examines one of Scotland's most shocking murders, and the impact it had on the people living there.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: In 1994, a gang kill a woman who manages a building society and tie up her husband, before robbing the building society of large sums of cash.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: In 2015, a 69-year-old man is found stabbed in his home. CCTV at the bank reveals a woman using the victim's card to withdraw cash. How does she relate to the murder?

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: Throughout the early '90s young boys would be found dead after sniffing glue and being trapped in derelict buildings which caught fire.

programme.title: Britain's Darkest Taboos
programme.sub-title: My Mother: Murdered By Her Golden Boy Son
programme.desc: Ross Taggart dialled 999 on the 23rd December 2014 to report his mother Carol missing. Two weeks later he was charged with her murder. David Swindle and Emma examine how a son could commit such a callous act.

programme.title: Britain's Darkest Taboos
programme.desc: Family members talk, for the first time, about the murder of 12-year-old Tia Sharp by Stuart Hazell in London in 2012.

programme.title: Britain's Darkest Taboos
programme.sub-title: He Trapped Our Sons In The Attic, Then Set Fire To The House
programme.desc: On the 22nd October 2014 Darren Sykes lured his sons into the attic and then set fire to the house. Their mother Claire Throssell reveals how Darren's behaviour spiraled out of control and the devastating impact of his actions.

programme.title: Britain's Darkest Taboos
programme.sub-title: My Secret Torment: I Was Abused By My Father
programme.desc: Leanne Meecham was murdered in February 2014 by her abusive stepfather, Simon Meecham. Leanne's sister Carly and her friend Alex tell us about their deep sense of loss and David and Emma look at what drove Meecham to murder.

programme.title: Britain's Darkest Taboos
programme.sub-title: Three Generations Murdered In One Night
programme.desc: Explores the house fire started by Carl Mills in Wales in 2012, which killed three generations of the same family.

programme.title: Nightmare in Suburbia
programme.desc: Documentary examining how the threat of divorce forced a husband to kill his wife to save his farm. Although now behind bars, Adrian Prout still hasn't revealed where his dead wife lies.

programme.title: Nightmare in Suburbia

programme.desc: Philadelphia police officers find four adults locked in a filthy basement dungeon as the investigation leads detectives across four states, resulting in multiple felony arrests; all evidence points to a single ringleader behind a deadly scheme.

programme.title: The Addams Family
programme.desc: The lives of Gomez and Morticia Addams are thrown into disarray when a scheming siren attempts to pass off her son as Gomez's long-lost brother Fester. Once installed in the family's spooky mansion, mother and son set their minds on stealing the family fortune.

programme.title: Godzilla vs. Kong
programme.desc: Fearsome monsters Godzilla and Kong square off in an epic battle for the ages. The initial confrontation between the two titans -- instigated by unseen forces -- is only the beginning of the mystery that lies deep within the core of the planet.

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: News from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Godzilla vs. Kong
programme.desc: Fearsome monsters Godzilla and Kong square off in an epic battle for the ages. The initial confrontation between the two titans -- instigated by unseen forces -- is only the beginning of the mystery that lies deep within the core of the planet.

programme.title: The Only Way Is Essex
programme.desc: Drama unfolds at a dinner party, Amber is determined to clear her name amid the ongoing conflict, and Dan and Ella cross paths for the first time since Vietnam.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois becomes a black belt while working out the aggression she feels toward the leaf-peeping New Yorkers who flock to Quahog during autumn.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg, who has a crush on newscaster Tom Tucker, applies for an internship at the local television station and gets the job. Meanwhile, Stewie loses his tricycle to a bully.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A gallery owner admires Chris' painting and invites the Griffins to New York. Elsewhere, Peter envies an old classmate.

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan has his heart set on speaking at the Republican National Convention on behalf of the Langley chapter.

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: After receiving a hefty bonus from work, Stan buys extravagant gadgets while Francine pleads for her dream kiosk. Upset about his lack of support, Francine opens a muffin shop without his approval and gives Stan a taste of life without a housewife. In a desperate move, Stan puts Klaus' brain back into a human body, but Klaus tries to win over Francine.

programme.title: G'Wed
programme.desc: Reece, inspired by Christopher, tries to track down his dad that he has never met. Aimee and Mia-Louise fall out big time over their choice of boyfriend.

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: The boys set off on a wine-buying trip to Tuscany. But when their cart has a collision on the road, they're forced to pull in to a nearby village. There, Jason hooks up with local milk maid Calypso and Grumio develops a taste for foraged truffles.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Join Stephen Mulhern as Ryan gets his opportunity to take on the Banker - he may choose to take an offer from the Banker in exchange for what's in his box or hold out until the end and be rewarded.

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Arthur decide to splash out on a new loo.

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Morse and Lewis investigate a baffling murder in a multi-story car park where the only clue is a diary and a parking ticket. A jealous husband, an unfaithful wife and a playboy architect add up to murder.

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Inspector Morse and Sergeant Lewis focus their attention on a respectable academic family as they investigate the death of an eminent scientist - attacked just before he was due to deliver a controversial speech on the environment.

programme.title: Rosemary and Thyme
programme.desc: Rosemary and Laura are employed at Regent's Park to recreate the original Victorian planting in the Avenue Garden, but the beauty and peace of the Avenue is shattered when a Harley Street psychiatrist is found murdered. Rosemary and Laura adopt the dead man's three-legged dog, who seems to have a more than a canine understanding of events.

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Jacko Argyle, convicted of murdering his adoptive mother Rachel, went to the gallows protesting his innocence. His family are shocked when some months later, stranger Dr Arthur Calgary proves that he did indeed have an alibi: he was with him at the time of the murder.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Mysterious lights are seen in the sky over famous UFO hot-spot Cooper Hill. When a local forest ranger suffers a strange death, the visiting UFO spotters are convinced aliens are responsible. DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets in their search for the truth.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An international cycling competition comes to the Midsomer village of Burwood Mantle. When the race leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson find themselves drawn into a ruthlessly competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed where winning comes at any cost.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Art comes to the picturesque Midsomer village of Angel's Rise with the opening of a new sculpture park. When its launch is marred by murder, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson have to get creative if they are to crack a case where art imitates death, and everything has a deeper meaning.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a Saint's bones at an archaeological dig causes a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. But when the leader of the dig is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson soon realise that it is not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Vera Stanhope nets two cases when an ex-prisoner's body is discovered on the banks of the Tyne, and must find out if the former inmate been served cold justice, or whether his death runs deeper.

programme.title: Joan
programme.desc: . As Boisie and Albie conduct reconnaissance on Bernard's shop, Joan attends Kelly's seventh birthday feeling a growing distance from her and visits her dying father hoping that he will apologise for abusing her as a child.

programme.title: Joan
programme.desc: It's the day before the heist and Joan is preparing to gain access to Kelly's school.

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Brian Moore introduces coverage of a derby day in September 1975 as Bobby Moore's Fulham entertain West London rivals Chelsea at a sodden Craven Cottage, Manchester City host neighbours Manchester United in a pulsating game, and Aston Villa take on Birmingham City in the Midlands.

programme.title: FIM Superbike World Championship Motorcycle Racing
programme.desc: The 2026 World Superbike Championship continues with the Czech Round as the 4.212km track at the Autodrom Most pushes the riders to their limits as they look to get points on the board.

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley are tasked with championing a decade-defining supercar released in 1992.

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship LIVE
programme.desc: The 2026 British Touring Car Championship season continues with Rounds 7, 8 and 9 at the Snetterton circuit. Following a dominant start to the season, four-time champion Ash Sutton tops the drivers' standings, with a commanding 47-point lead over longstanding rival Tom Ingram. Nicki Shields presents live coverage from Norfolk, alongside expert pundits Tim Harvey, Paul O'Neill and Jade Edwards. Reporting from the pit lane is Louise Goodman, and commentary comes from David Addison.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Failing trailer brakes and trash talk over the CB threaten to derail Lisa's comeback, and newcomer Greg races to reignite his rivalry with Jack, before a devastating wreck slams the brakes on Greg's need for speed.

programme.title: Tomorrow Never Dies
programme.desc: A media baron sets about triggering World War III so that his 24-hour news network can profit from exclusive coverage of the conflict. As an international crisis looms, James Bond is shaken from his extracurricular duties and stirred into action.

programme.desc: Action from the League One Play-Off final at Wembley Stadium, as one team reached the Championship, while it was heartbreak at the final hurdle for the losers.

programme.title: Under Siege
programme.desc: A heroic loner takes on a group of nuclear terrorists in this seagoing yarn. Posing as a rock band, the terrorists get themselves hired for a party aboard the USS Missouri, a battleship en route to Pearl Harbor for decommissioning. They plan to steal the ship's nuclear arsenal but haven't reckoned on the intervention of the ship's chef, a decorated former Navy SEAL.

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Lynn Hurst is injured in a road accident. In the ambulance she produces a gun and escapes. Regan and Carter soon discover she was part of a gang behind the wages theft at the Local Education Authority.

programme.title: BattleBots
programme.desc: This episode sees the return of Deathroll - the 'Aussie Assassin' that punches its blood-soaked fist in victory every time it destroys an opponent - as they face the British bot destroyer Cobalt, plus bot legend Donald Hutson coming back to try to resurrect Lock-Jaw's reputation after a disappointing display in the sixth series when he faces Bunny Sauriol and her fearsome bot Malice.

programme.title: Magnum, P.I.
programme.desc: It's a dog-eat-dog world out there, as a peculiar puppy attracts the attention of some vicious gangsters. Magnum gets his paws into this curious case.

programme.title: Magnum, P.I.
programme.desc: A woman with ESP believes her missing boyfriend is still around.

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Which man is which? That's the problem facing Simon Templar when a business tycoon claims he is being impersonated by a perfect double.

programme.title: Boon
programme.sub-title: A Ride on the Wild Side
programme.desc: Boon narrowly escapes appearing in pornographic film, and Debbie's younger sister is kidnapped.

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Leonard Gold, a wealthy self-made tycoon, is being blackmailed for his part in the massacre of a Malay village, while serving as a Corporal in the Army. With the theft of the regimental report and a number of fake solicitor's letters, the Flying Squad are called in.

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur opens `The Daley Post', an all new London dispatch service. It isn't long before his competitors decide that he should be the one who has lots of business, not all of it being kosher.

programme.title: Smokey and the Bandit II
programme.desc: Folk hero the Bandit has fallen on bad times but comes to life when he is hired to help transport a pregnant elephant to a Republican convention in Dallas. Arch-enemy Sheriff Buford T Justice catches wind of the deal and resolves to spoil the Bandit's comeback.

programme.desc: A chill is sent through the camp this week when Rousseau appears carrying a rifle, claiming that `The Others' are coming. Just as escape seems impossible, the island's volcano erupts but can boll and Bintang escape its fury?

Advanced computer simulations reveal exactly what caused France's worst train crash.

programme.desc: Peppa and George are at the playground with their friends. Peppa loves to play on the swings, but George is a bit afraid of heights and doesn't like to be pushed too high.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Pig has a lovely new picture of Peppa and George, so Daddy Pig offers to put it up, but it's not as simple as Daddy Pig thought, and he makes a very big hole in the wall.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George go to the beach and have fun burying Daddy Pig in the sand.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: When George finds a spider in the bathroom, Peppa is very frightened until she realises that it doesn't want to hurt her.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny and Grandpa Pig have a beautiful little boat, and Peppa and George are spending the day on the river with them.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Pig, Daddy Pig, Peppa and George go to the supermarket, and George sits in the trolley while Peppa finds the things on Mummy's shopping list.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It's Peppa Pig's birthday so she awakens very early. First, Peppa gets a present from Mummy, Daddy and George, and then it's time for her party. Daddy Pig does some funny magic tricks and makes a birthday cake appear with four candles for Peppa to blow out.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mom, Dad, Peppa and George decide to go to the beach on a beautiful sunny day. Suddenly, a little cloud covers the sun for a minute, but then it passes and the sun reappears.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa's playgroup is putting on a play, and Peppa is going to be Little Red Riding Hood.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and Rebecca Rabbit pretend to be babies to get Mummy Rabbit's attention. Mummy Rabbit plays along until they admit they are not really babies!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: PC Panda brings his twin daughters to playgroup Peggi and Pandora Panda. They love puzzles and to solve mysteries just like their dad.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa's family are at Digger World, where they fun building walls and knocking them over again, and even help Mr Bull when there's an obstacle in the road.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and the rest of the playgroup put on a big Christmas show for all the Mummies and Daddies. While Danny Dog is dressed up as a Santa, the real Father Christmas pays them a visit!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa takes Goldie the Goldfish for a visit to the vets and is surprised when she finds that there's a new road to be built over the building! With the help of Mr. Bull and his brother they find that instead of demolishing the vets they can build a tunnel underneath it.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Daddy Pig has a book from the library that he has kept for too long. The family go to the library to return it and find more books to read.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends are playing hide and seek, but it is Freddy Fox with his super smelling nose who is the best at playing that game.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa tries to learn how to whistle after she realises that seemingly everyone but her can do it.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Daddy Pig loses his title as Champion Puddle Jumper, until everyone helps him train to win it back.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George realise that they have shadows and no matter what they do, they can't escape from them.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family win a trip to America to star in Super Potato's movie, `Vegetables in Space'. Miss Rabbit flies them over and they land in New York only to meet a new Miss Rabbit who shows them around the city of New York. They go up the Empire State Building and see the lights in Times Square. Now they have to get to Hollywood.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family are driving in their large campervan across America to get to Hollywood. They stop at a diner to ask for directions and meet another Miss Rabbit. She sings them some songs and cooks them some eggs, over easy, of course, then they go to a barn dance followed closely by watching a Monster Truck rally, expect lots of muddy puddles.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family continue driving across America to get to Hollywood. As they drive through the desert, they meet Mr. Buffalo and Mr. Bison who are doing road work just like Mr. Bull from back home. They meet another Miss Rabbit and fly over the Grand Canyon in a helicopter, white water raft through the rapids and discover a cave from 700AD.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family make it to Hollywood, but they need to find the movie studio where Super Potato and Hash Brown are making the film `Vegetables in Space'. They get lost on the freeway, but another Miss Rabbit tells them to follow the stars, which turn out to be stars in the pavement.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: At playgroup, Madame Gazelle teaches Peppa and friends how to exercise with hoops, or hulahoop. Peppa takes her hoop home and teaches Mummy and Daddy how to use it.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy, Daddy, Peppa and George go to the park where they meet their friends, swing on the monkey bars, and bounce on a bouncy branch. They then end with jumping in lots of muddy puddles.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Pig shows Peppa how to play pretend by teaching her how to wink. Zoe Zebra comes to play with Peppa and brings along her toy monkey. Peppa pretends Monkey has a cough.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Police Officer Panda and Police Officer Squirrel visit Peppa's playgroup. They offer two lucky children the chance to spend the day with them.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends play some very active games in the garden and together they learn to hop, skip and jump.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Miss Rabbit's Rescue Service has saved many people from tricky situations, but what happens when Miss Rabbit needs rescuing herself.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa learns that Mummy Pig used to keep a diary when she was her age, so Peppa decides to give it a try.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: George and Peppa spend the day playing golf with Grandpa Pig, but they are much more interested in the colourful minigolf course next door.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The playgroup visits a nature reserve, specialising in tiny insects, bugs and butterflies. So Pedro brings along his pet stick insect.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.desc: Mummy, Daddy, Peppa and George go to the park; Peppa and George play on the hopping logs, the bouncing branch and the monkey bars.

programme.desc: Peppa celebrates Goldie's birthday by holding a undersea party - by turning the house into an underwater environment.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends are at their clubhouse. They decide to start a detective club, and when Daddy Pig loses his car keys, they have a real mystery to solve.

programme.desc: Mr Bull builds a brand-new club house and slide for Peppa and her friends.

programme.desc: Lotte Llama joins the playgroup, and teaches the others how to speak German.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa, her friends, and their parents, learn woodworking from Mister Wolf. He asks Daddy Pig to make a hut out of twigs, but it isn't very strong and Mister Wolf manages to blow it down.

programme.desc: Peppa looks after Madame Gazelle's guinea pigs Ginger and Brian while she goes on holiday, but prove to be more trouble for Mummy and Daddy Pig than they think.

programme.desc: Peppa, George and Daddy Pig visit the charity shop to sell things they don't need, and things they may need.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends make up stories about their families. Even Penny gets a picture too.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa, George, Grandpa and Granny go owl-spotting.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The family visit the apple tree, and notice how it changes every season as they keep visiting it.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George find themselves struggling to walk up the big hill.

programme.desc: Peppa and George check out Grandpa's new Bug Hotel.

programme.desc: The Playgroup play many games using a parachute.

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mr. Potato visits the playgroup to teach the children a Kiddie Workout with many differnt exercises.

programme.desc: Peppa and her friends visit the Little Cars Play Centre where they get to drive little toy cars.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family team up with Danny Dog to swing across the monkey trees.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family hire a canoe from Miss Rabbit. They imagine they are in a jungle and then unexpectedly bump into Mrs Crocodile. Later on, Mr Lion invites them to have a picnic.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The adventures of Peppa Pig and friends continue with more fun and games.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family are going to the Water Park. Miss Rabbit is in charge. There are lots of fun things to do at the water park but first you need to put on your swimsuit.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George have come to play at Granny and Grandpa's house. Everyone dresses as dinosaurs having lots of fun and a bit of a party.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Pedro loves superheroes. He has a superhero-themed party and Super Potato is one of the guests.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa Pig and Suzy Sheep are spending the day with Doctor Hamster, and they are learning about what vets do. Doctor Hamster says she's glad to have two helpers with her and calls the next patient.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family go bowling at the Peppatown Bowling Alley.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Miss Rabbit is teaching the children a swimming lesson, They start out by swimming with floats tucked under their arms.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa Pig discovers what a cruise ship is, and goes on a cruise to a country called Greece.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: There are lots of activities to do on the cruise, like breakfast at the pirate cafe. There, they meet Pirate Pete and Mrs Mermaid who invite everyone to their show.

programme.desc: Peppa and George play at Granny and Grandpa Pig's house. Grandma, Peppa and George go to the shed to see what Grandpa is doing and discover that he's built a robot.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends learn how to animate their drawings at playgroup.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family renovate their home.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: With the building work complete, it's time to decorate! Peppa and George have fun choosing how to decorate their new attic bedrooms. It's dinosaurs for George, but what will Peppa pick?

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa's family car breaks down. While it is being repaired, they borrow a shiny new one from Grandad Dog's garage.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny and Grandpa Pig are taking Peppa and George to a festival - a jigsaw festival! Peppa is amazed by all of the completed jigsaws around the festival.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Madame Gazelle is making a wicker basket. She explains it is her hobby - something you do to relax. The children discuss hobbies and learn about each other's favourite things to do.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Pig and Peppa are at Granny Pig's house. They find Mummy's old patchwork quilt that Granny made for her as a baby. Peppa decides she'd like to make one for the baby!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is out with Suzy and Granny Sheep in Mummy Sheep's car. They come across an old van that Miss Rabbit is recycling. Granny Sheep decides she would like to turn it into an art van.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny Sheep is driving Vanessa, the art van along the road with Peppa, Suzy, Pedro, Mandy, Molly and Danny. They are taking her to Grampy Rabbit's boat yard, for her first-ever outing!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: While Mummy and Daddy Pig attend a refresher antenatal class, Peppa and George join Miss Rabbit's Sibling School where they learn about what to expect when their new sibling arrives.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is so excited to meet her new baby brother or sister, but they are taking forever to arrive until suddenly Mummy Pig announces the baby is coming, now!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is determined to find the perfect name for the baby.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The nurse is popping around, and she decides that today's visit should be all about how to hold a baby. The nurse shows Peppa a special way for a small person to safely hold a baby.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George play at Granny and Grandpa Pig's house. Grandma, Peppa and George go to the shed to see what Grandpa is doing and discover that he's built a robot.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It's a snowy day and Peppa's family build an igloo for their snowman. They hide in it during a snowstorm, but Penny Polar Bear and her parents love snowstorms - and also Peppa's igloo!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends are at playgroup when they learn that Mr. Bull and Mrs. Cow are getting married the next day; everyone goes into a panic when they confess how they have done nothing to prepare for the big day.

programme.desc: Everyone prepares for Mr. Bull and Mrs. Cow's wedding; Peppa and the soon-to-be-weds go to the jewellers to get rings, to the dress makers, to get a wedding dress and finally to Peppa's house to sort the wedding cake.

programme.desc: The day of Mrs. Cow and Mr. Bull wedding arrives, but not everything goes to plan; nothing stops it being the best wedding ever and there is a lot of happy crying.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It's a snowy day and Peppa's family build an igloo for their snowman. They hide in it during a snowstorm, but Penny Polar Bear and her parents love snowstorms - and also Peppa's igloo!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends are at playgroup when they learn that Mr. Bull and Mrs. Cow are getting married the next day; everyone goes into a panic when they confess how they have done nothing to prepare for the big day.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Everyone prepares for Mr. Bull and Mrs. Cow's wedding; Peppa and the soon-to-be-weds go to the jewellers to get rings, to the dress makers, to get a wedding dress and finally to Peppa's house to sort the wedding cake.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The day of Mrs. Cow and Mr. Bull wedding arrives, but not everything goes to plan; nothing stops it being the best wedding ever and there is a lot of happy crying.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa receives an invite to Candy Cat's birthday party, but a double-decker party bus arrives; the bus goes to the playground and even the beach, hosting the best party ever.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mom, Dad, Peppa and George decide to go to the beach on a beautiful sunny day. Suddenly, a little cloud covers the sun for a minute, but then it passes and the sun reappears.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is on a play date with Candy Cat at Mr Cat's Cafe. Candy show Mr Cat the drawings they've done of everyone, and he hangs them up for everyone to see.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: On a cold day, Daddy Pig suggests they build a den until Mummy Pig has finished work. After a hard day's work, the cushion den is a perfect place to watch TV together!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family are watching TV... until it breaks! While Grampy Rabbit fixes it, the family get a new TV which can do everything... but it doesn't beat their old TV.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A storm cancels Peppa and her family's adventure plans so they decide to take it inside. Using their imagination, they go sledding, climb a mountain and have a campfire in the attic.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George are playing in their pool. They help the garden animals cool down but when it rains, the animals are happy and so is Peppa. Rain makes muddy puddles for jumping in!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George are in Granny and Grandpa Pig's Garden. Granny's friends are playing folk music! They play music for Grandpa Pig and then Grandpa Pig makes apple juice for everyone!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It is bedtime and Peppa calls Kylie Kangaroo and is surprised to learn that it is not morning or winter where she is! Daddy Pig explains this using a big ball to represent the Earth.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: While visiting Suzy and Emily's house, Peppa is surprised that there are extra rules which she does not have in her own house. On the drive home, Peppa explains to Mummy Pig that different houses have different rules.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Pig is confirming she is having a new baby. Mummy and Daddy Pig share the exciting news with Peppa and her brother, George.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family are on the search for a new home.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family renovate their home.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: With the building work complete, it's time to decorate! Peppa and George have fun choosing how to decorate their new attic bedrooms. It's dinosaurs for George, but what will Peppa pick?

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa's family car breaks down. While it is being repaired, they borrow a shiny new one from Grandad Dog's garage.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny and Grandpa Pig are taking Peppa and George to a festival - a jigsaw festival! Peppa is amazed by all of the completed jigsaws around the festival.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Madame Gazelle is making a wicker basket. She explains it is her hobby - something you do to relax. The children discuss hobbies and learn about each other's favourite things to do.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Pig and Peppa are at Granny Pig's house. They find Mummy's old patchwork quilt that Granny made for her as a baby. Peppa decides she'd like to make one for the baby!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is out with Suzy and Granny Sheep in Mummy Sheep's car. They come across an old van that Miss Rabbit is recycling. Granny Sheep decides she would like to turn it into an art van.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny Sheep is driving Vanessa, the art van along the road with Peppa, Suzy, Pedro, Mandy, Molly and Danny. They are taking her to Grampy Rabbit's boat yard, for her first-ever outing!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: While Mummy and Daddy Pig attend a refresher antenatal class, Peppa and George join Miss Rabbit's Sibling School where they learn about what to expect when their new sibling arrives.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is so excited to meet her new baby brother or sister, but they are taking forever to arrive until suddenly Mummy Pig announces the baby is coming, now!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is determined to find the perfect name for the baby.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The nurse is popping around, and she decides that today's visit should be all about how to hold a baby. The nurse shows Peppa a special way for a small person to safely hold a baby.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: On a day out with her family, Peppa is encouraged to find words to describe the different kinds of puddles they find.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mandy Mouse joins Peppa's playgroup. She uses a wheelchair and is super sporty. The children have a lot of fun playing together.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Madame Gazelle teaches the children how to play the recorder. They practise a lot at home, then put on a performance for all the parents to enjoy.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Miss Rabbit persuades Mummy Pig to go to her relaxation class, but relaxing proves more difficult than expected. However, thanks to Daddy Pig's bouncy castle, they all have fun in the end.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: When Daddy Pig is given a parking ticket, Peppa and George learn that adults have rules just like children - and that they get told off too.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A new synthesiser arrives for Daddy Pig, and his family collect sounds for it from around the house while he is out at work.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: During a picnic in her garden, cousin Chloe shows Peppa and George fun things to do with flowers.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Rebecca and Richard Rabbit are playing a marble run game at Peppa's house. But when Daddy Pig joins in and improvises tunnels and bridges, the marble rolls out of the bedroom, causing chaos and nearly knocking Goldie out of her bowl.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: When George loses the metal wind-up key for his toy robot in the long grass, Grandpa Pig cannot find his metal detector to help George track it down.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: For World Book Day, the playgroup must dress up as a character from their favourite book. But Peppa can't decide which as she has so many favourites.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa, George, Mummy and Daddy all go on a lovely trip to the children's festival. There is lots to see and do, including singing, making crafts and listening to stories.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Day two at the festival and it has rained all night. The festival is very muddy and Peppa is thrilled. Luckily, it rains every year and Miss Rabbit is prepared with lots of muddy activities.

programme.desc: Peppa and Suzy are excited about the sleepover and sit up in bed talking about what they will do when they grow up.
