programme.desc: Tracey has had enough of Dorien and Sharon. She is annoyed by the way Dorien continues entertaining her gentlemen callers and is irritated by the fact that Sharon is not bringing in a living wage - so she throws them both out. Holed up back in Sharon's old bedsit, the two of them decide to join forces and take a united stand - but can they?

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.sub-title: Tonight I'll Be Staying Here with You
programme.desc: Auntie Vera has got problems with her hips and her defective lifts and tells Sharon and Tracey she is moving in. There is only got one room available though, and a dismayed Dorien is told that family must come first.

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: A surprise visitor in the form of attractive Emma turns up at Tracey's house. Tracey is welcoming, Dorien is shocked to the core, and Travis is over the moon.

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Travis has serious school news that he is scared to break to his mum. Sharon decides that she can pass herself off as Tracey and sort out his problems - but her efforts land the family in a mess that only arch-seducer Dorien can get them out of.

programme.title: Steptoe and Son
programme.desc: Albert and Harold are both preparing for Christmas, but Harold's plans do not include his father. In fact, he is determined to get as far away from him as possible and has plane tickets for Majorca.

The new military arrival soon discovers that the disruptions in the camp are merely a cover for an imminent breakout attempt.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to boost the British economy by embarking on a trade mission to India. Equipped with three old British cars and a range of uniquely British products, they set off on an epic road trip across one of the world's most fascinating and challenging countries, devotedly promoting Britain with distinctly mixed results.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In this special Christmas episode, Freddie, Chris and Paddy play 'car secret Santa' for a festive road trip across Britain, transporting a giant Christmas tree as they head to Top Gear's very own winter wonderland.

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Gavin starts his new job in Cardiff and Stacey is thrilled to be at home again. Smithy comes to terms with life in Essex without his best mate, whilst Nessa is adjusting to life in Dave's caravan down Sully. The weekend brings with it a big reunion, as everyone meets up for the christening of Neil the baby, but how will Smithy react to news from Nessa that it is a double celebration?

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Stacey is trying for a new job whilst ensuring that Gavin sticks to his recent promise, and Dave is not a happy bunny when Nessa tells him she is taking Neil the baby to Essex to see his dad. Meanwhile, over at the Shipman's house, Dawn and Pete have joined Pam and Mick for a beer and curry night of huge proportions - at the Billericay house of fun.

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Pam and Mick are having second thoughts about the conservatory, whilst Bryn is in his element as he awaits the arrival of his houseguests - all the Essex boys down for a big night out in Cardiff - and having a sleep over at his! Elsewhere, Smithy gets in a state about seeing Nessa after their most recent embarrassing meeting, and Stacey makes a worrying discovery, but is telling Gavin the right thing to do?

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Gavin is uncomfortable with Gwen and Bryn knowing the ins and outs of his and Stacey's sex life, and Smithy goes beyond the call of friendship by making a staggering suggestion. Meanwhile, Pete and Dawn prepare to renew their wedding vows, and everyone heads to Billericay to help them celebrate. But how will Dave react to the bombshell revelation about the curry night in Essex?

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: It is Bank Holiday Monday, and the Essex contingent arrives for a sunny day out at the beach; even Doris gets her sun dress on. Gavin's heart-to-heart with his dad gives him something more to think about, and a dark cloud looms on the horizon when Dave arrives to find Nessa and Smithy enjoying all the fun of the fair.

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Nessa and Dave's wedding is fast approaching, and the preparations are in full swing. Stacey breaks some life-changing news to Gavin, while Bryn and Jason finally put their big secret to bed. Smithy takes care of Neil the baby, and wishes Nessa all the best as she makes her way to the church to marry Dave.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for previously unseen material, and a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal amazing stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Among the guests joining Lee Mack and David Mitchell are James Acaster, David Baddiel, Ed Balls, Mark Bonnar, Jo Brand, Steve Davis, Susie Dent, Ade Edmondson, Sheila Hancock, Claude Littner, Cariad Lloyd, Gabby Logan, Stephen Mangan, Bob Mortimer, Melvin Odoom, and Ore Oduba.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob return to the Peak District. They start by fishing the Wye at Haddon Hall, hoping to catch chub, rainbow trout and wild brown trout, before moving on to the Derwent. As they cast, they recall memories of their first trip.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob, Paul and Ted travel to Scotland to taste the traditional Hogmanay celebrations it has to offer. As well as fishing the rivers Dee, Tay and Ericht, they will be joined by an old friend for dinner, and a very special guest performs.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers: Mums Know Best
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers are treated to flamboyant recipes that would be fit for a king. Si King and Dave Myers sample such delights as a pig's trotter dish from the Baltics and a lemon souffle by order of the royal family. They learn the secret behind a perfect baked Alaska and conjure up their own prized honey glazed ham. The lads are also shown a lesson or two in etiquette.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers: Mums Know Best
programme.desc: On your one special day, you deserve the best dish on the menu and who better to steer you in the right direction than the Hairy Bikers? Join Si King and Dave Myers as they visit three mums and discover what they hold dearest for the day of many happy returns. The lads rustle up a summer berry trifle, while our mums treat the boys to such varied dishes as a Manchester tart and greek stuffed vine leaves.

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve travels from the islands of the Aegean to Greece's capital, Athens. To learn more about Greece and the Greeks, he meets an extraordinary cast of characters, from gun-toting priests to the last remaining sponge divers. Getting behind the postcard image of this beautiful country, he finds out how the Greeks are coming to terms with a seemingly endless crisis.

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve travels from the Peloponnese peninsula to the rugged and mountainous north of the country. To learn more about Greece and the Greeks, he meets an extraordinary cast of characters, from a group of rebel monks to conservationists caring for an injured bear cub.

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales revisit the Kennet and Avon Canal in the West Country. Back in 1990, to promote the fully restored canal, they were invited to be on the first boat in 42 years to travel the full length of the K&A.

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley examines Agatha Christie's later life, and discovers how, amid the turbulent social and political change of the 1930s and 40s, newfound personal happiness ushered in a golden age for her writing.

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The fairytale hills of Guilin and the cormorant fishermen of the Li River form the heart of this exploration of the colourful rice-growing cultures and strange creatures of southern China - a land of endless hills, mysterious caverns, spectacular rock pinnacles and traditional cultures with a taste for wildlife.

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Beneath billowing clouds, in China's far south west, rich jungles nestle below towering peaks. Jewel-coloured birds and ancient tribes share forested valleys where wild elephants still roam. These remote forests stretch into northern territories where normally deserts would be found. How do these forests exist? Perhaps the rugged landscape holds the key.

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Exploring the vast Tibetan plateau, the home to a remarkable culture shaped by over 1,000 years of Buddhism and mountains and glaciers that provide a vital life support system for half the planet.

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley examines Agatha Christie's later life, and discovers how, amid the turbulent social and political change of the 1930s and 40s, newfound personal happiness ushered in a golden age for her writing.

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The fairytale hills of Guilin and the cormorant fishermen of the Li River form the heart of this exploration of the colourful rice-growing cultures and strange creatures of southern China - a land of endless hills, mysterious caverns, spectacular rock pinnacles and traditional cultures with a taste for wildlife.

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Beneath billowing clouds, in China's far south west, rich jungles nestle below towering peaks. Jewel-coloured birds and ancient tribes share forested valleys where wild elephants still roam. These remote forests stretch into northern territories where normally deserts would be found. How do these forests exist? Perhaps the rugged landscape holds the key.

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Exploring the vast Tibetan plateau, the home to a remarkable culture shaped by over 1,000 years of Buddhism and mountains and glaciers that provide a vital life support system for half the planet.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Preparations are afoot in the Harper household for the arrival of Sylvie, a French exchange student. Nick is keen to establish `entente cordiale' - preferably in his bedroom, but Janey is wary of the `Parisian beauty'. Meanwhile, Ben meets a prospective employee who does little to put him at ease.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Parents Ben and Susan reluctantly agree to let daughter Janey have a party whilst they are away on holiday - and plan to sneak back unnoticed into the house and check up on the children, just to be on the safe side.

programme.title: My Family
programme.sub-title: Death and Ben Take a Holiday
programme.desc: Susan, Ben and the children travel to a family funeral in Leeds. In addition to reservation problems at the hotel, Nick's shameless liaisons with the hotel maid and Susan's inappropriate behaviour at the funeral add up to a fraught weekend.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben is distressed as he faces trouble at work in the shape of his new assistant, who cannot stand the sight of blood. His troubles do not get any easier when he arrives home and must face the trauma of his neighbour's DIY antics. Meanwhile, Janey's preparations for her driving test end with an unforgettable lesson with her dad.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The Harpers plan to redecorate their house, however, with Nick taking instructions from `The Naked Chef' rather too literally, and Janey's boyfriend spending the night in her room without her parents' permission, it appears that work will never get started.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Nick has a new girlfriend and the family get a bit of a shock when he brings her round for dinner. Also, Michael gets a new computer and Janey is starting to feel like the `poor relation'.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.sub-title: Merida, Tito, and Monti - Puppies Special
programme.desc: Stories, old and new, of some of the youngest and cutest puppies for whom Noel has provided life-saving treatment, including cockapoo Monti who fractured his knee and Weimaraner Tito who was hit by a van.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats Boston terrier Rogan's debilitating dysplasia and creates an implant for Hungarian vizsla Brodie. And Vinnie the cat has been hit by a car and damaged his back legs.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Marbella-based, French bulldog Milo is flown in by private jet to get his broken elbow fixed, and Maine Coon-mad Noel helps cool cat Digby, who has severe hip dysplasia.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Britain's favourite vet, Prof Noel Fitzpatrick, travels to South Africa to help some of the most majestic but endangered wild animals, including lame tiger Laziz who has crushing arthritis.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Clarissa and Charles bring their blue Russian cat Maya to the surgery, after a dog attack has left her with a shattered jaw. Chris, who is living in a homeless shelter, brings in his much-loved best friend Spud, a Labrador cross with a lame back leg.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to help a family involved in a car crash on a dark street and reassure a woman with dementia who's having a suspected heart attack.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews require support from the fire brigade to rescue a woman who's fallen and is stuck inside her locked house. Plus, they help a child with a dangerously high temperature.

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Lyn has an investigation of her womb after a recent biopsy revealed abnormal cells and Steve is worried about only getting a local anaesthetic before his varicose veins are removed.

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Julia has a camera investigation of her womb to find out the reason for her heavy bleeding and Emma has a section of bone removed from her foot to cure a painful condition.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Sandra Graham combines her job in the city with the children's playground game "Last Hit"; Helen is devastated to hear that her husband has not managed to find any brie.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: The fifth in the hit comedy sketch series featuring a gallery of characters from the 'Doctor Who' actress. Here, the foul-mouthed granny discovers a pound shop.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: The last in the first series of the award-winning comedy sketch show co-written and performed by the versatile comedy actress. Are you bovvered?

programme.title: Who Killed Jenni Rivera?
programme.desc: An exploration of the mysterious death of Latin superstar Jenni Rivera through intimate footage and interviews, delving into the potential causes and mysteries of her horrific 2012 plane crash.

programme.title: Deadly Waters With Captain Lee
programme.desc: Three missing fingers point to the identity of a body found off the coast of Catalina Island, plunging authorities into the mysterious investigation of a wealthy mogul, luxurious yacht and a manhunt for a killer hiding in an unlikely place.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews...
programme.desc: Louis spends time with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life, from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, as well as the pressures that come with being one of the country's best-loved musicians and role models.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Dudley, a man collapses while playing bingo two days before his wedding. Meanwhile, in Stoke, the crew are called out to help an elderly man who has had an unexplained fall.

programme.desc: The Harpers plan to redecorate their house, however, with Nick taking instructions from `The Naked Chef' rather too literally, and Janey's boyfriend spending the night in her room without her parents' permission, it appears that work will never get started.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Nick has a new girlfriend and the family get a bit of a shock when he brings her round for dinner. Also, Michael gets a new computer and Janey is starting to feel like the `poor relation'.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.sub-title: Merida, Tito, and Monti - Puppies Special
programme.desc: Stories, old and new, of some of the youngest and cutest puppies for whom Noel has provided life-saving treatment, including cockapoo Monti who fractured his knee and Weimaraner Tito who was hit by a van.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats Boston terrier Rogan's debilitating dysplasia and creates an implant for Hungarian vizsla Brodie. And Vinnie the cat has been hit by a car and damaged his back legs.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Marbella-based, French bulldog Milo is flown in by private jet to get his broken elbow fixed, and Maine Coon-mad Noel helps cool cat Digby, who has severe hip dysplasia.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Britain's favourite vet, Prof Noel Fitzpatrick, travels to South Africa to help some of the most majestic but endangered wild animals, including lame tiger Laziz who has crushing arthritis.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Clarissa and Charles bring their blue Russian cat Maya to the surgery, after a dog attack has left her with a shattered jaw. Chris, who is living in a homeless shelter, brings in his much-loved best friend Spud, a Labrador cross with a lame back leg.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to help a family involved in a car crash on a dark street and reassure a woman with dementia who's having a suspected heart attack.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews require support from the fire brigade to rescue a woman who's fallen and is stuck inside her locked house. Plus, they help a child with a dangerously high temperature.

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Lyn has an investigation of her womb after a recent biopsy revealed abnormal cells and Steve is worried about only getting a local anaesthetic before his varicose veins are removed.

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Julia has a camera investigation of her womb to find out the reason for her heavy bleeding and Emma has a section of bone removed from her foot to cure a painful condition.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Sandra Graham combines her job in the city with the children's playground game "Last Hit"; Helen is devastated to hear that her husband has not managed to find any brie.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: The fifth in the hit comedy sketch series featuring a gallery of characters from the 'Doctor Who' actress. Here, the foul-mouthed granny discovers a pound shop.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: The last in the first series of the award-winning comedy sketch show co-written and performed by the versatile comedy actress. Are you bovvered?

programme.title: Who Killed Jenni Rivera?
programme.desc: An exploration of the mysterious death of Latin superstar Jenni Rivera through intimate footage and interviews, delving into the potential causes and mysteries of her horrific 2012 plane crash.

programme.title: Deadly Waters With Captain Lee
programme.desc: Three missing fingers point to the identity of a body found off the coast of Catalina Island, plunging authorities into the mysterious investigation of a wealthy mogul, luxurious yacht and a manhunt for a killer hiding in an unlikely place.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews...
programme.desc: Louis spends time with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life, from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, as well as the pressures that come with being one of the country's best-loved musicians and role models.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Dudley, a man collapses while playing bingo two days before his wedding. Meanwhile, in Stoke, the crew are called out to help an elderly man who has had an unexplained fall.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: Kicking off the competition is the one and only Jamie Oliver! 18 contestants have 90 minutes to create a secret weapon dish - and the winner gets the biggest advantage ever seen in MasterChef.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Coco, Sapphire and the Cheese Chis
programme.desc: Pete helps rescue six Chihuahuas from the home of a hoarder and witnesses the emotional handover of a pregnant pug and her sister.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Nugget, Tetley and the Police Pups
programme.desc: Pete meets Chow Chow Nugget who has a longer stay than planned, three-legged Tetley, and some Belgian Malinois puppies training to be service dogs.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Keira, a dog on the brink of starvation, and gentle old Jack Russell Paige, whose owner had to make the heartbreaking decision to give her up after 15 years.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete helps rehabilitate a group of ex-racing greyhounds who have never experienced life beyond the racetrack.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Staffy Boxer Cross Crumble, who's been at the centre in Basildon for over three years, stuck without a foster or permanent home in sight.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: It's Pete's first day at Dogs Trust, and he meets a pug facing a worrying surgery and pregnant dog who was seized at the border.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets a litter of puppies known as the Potato Pups, who were found abandoned in a crisp box, and helps the staff fight to keep them alive.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets the four-legged friends that call the UK's leading dog welfare charity, Dogs Trust, home. From when they are rescued, Pete plays an instrumental role in their rehabilitation process, right up until the point the dogs are safely rehomed.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete travels to Northern Ireland to pick up three stray dogs and bring them back with him to Essex. He also meets a Shar Pei who is in constant pain and may need surgery.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete returns to Dogs Trust, facing a suitcase of abandoned pups on day one and falling hard for Theo, a gentle Italian Mastiff with a serious heart condition.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine in the hope of winning a cash jackpot.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Veterinarian Noel Fitzpatrick is one of England's top animal doctors and the subject of 2010 documentary programme `The Bionic Vet', which showcased some of the work done at his Surrey clinic. On `The Supervet' Fitzpatrick and his staff treat some of the country's hardest-to-cure pets with cutting-edge treatments and surgery. The programme tells the emotional stories of the pets, their owners and the passionate team that push the boundaries of veterinary medicine to save the animals from life-threatening conditions.

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Ocala in Florida to meet Jennifer Mee, once famous across the world as the Hiccup Girl and now notorious as a murderer serving life in prison.

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Gadsden Correctional Facility near Tallahassee in Florida where he meets Ashley Humphrey, who pumped eight bullets into a woman she had never met.

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A sideshow circus wife, whose dangerous occupation demands precision, swaps lives with a frazzled mother of four, whose home life is like a three-ring circus.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews...
programme.desc: Louis meets Oscar-winning actress Dame Judi Dench. Having graced the stage and screen for over 60 years, including iconic turns as Queen Elizabeth in Shakespeare in Love and MI5 chief M in Bond, Judi is regarded as one of Britain's greatest ever actors. Louis meets Judi at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre and her life away from acting.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Jo and Ash deal with a patient whose chronic cough leaves him struggling to breathe, while Joel and Nic treat a man who's fallen from a ladder while wielding a hedge trimmer.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Stepping into the Makers' Marquee today are glass-blower Mark from north Wales and potter Victor from London. Mark's love of glass-blowing started when he was a boy, but exposure to lead poisoning made him give up the craft he loved.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Artist Jack from Dorset and potter Jeanet from Durham step into Dom's unique makers' training camp, hoping to fulfil their dreams of making a living from their passions.

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his 1913 Bradshaw's Guide, Michael Portillo crosses the south east of Australia from the seat of government in Canberra to the multiethnic heart of its second largest city, Melbourne. Floating above Canberra in a hot air balloon, Michael sees how Australia's `Bush Capital' was planned in the early 20th century.

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From tropical rainforest to the Great Barrier Reef, Michael Portillo navigates his way by rail through Queensland, Australia, with the aid of his 1913 Bradshaw's Guide. Inching his way across the Stony Creek Bridge on the Kuranda Scenic Railway, he marvels at the engineering expertise of the 1890s, when the line was built, and the magnificent scenery 300 metres below him.

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is on the eastern coast of Australia travelling from the coal mines of Newcastle, New South Wales, to Queensland's modern state capital, Brisbane. Along the way, he crosses the mighty Hawkesbury River to admire magnificent scenery, tends to an injured koala, gets some tips from an Aussie rugby legend and rides a solar-powered train.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie begins a new canal adventure in Wales and encounters towering aqueducts and stunning scenery along the way.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie's journey on the Llangollen Canal takes an unexpected turn when his boat gets stuck in silt and a passing hire boat comes to the rescue.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: S6/Ep1 - Robbie Cumming navigates the scenic Oxford Canal onboard Naughty Lass, explores its twists and turns, and helps tow a stranded broken-down boater.

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Adventurer and journalist Simon Reeve heads to Cuba to find a communist country in the middle of a capitalist revolution. In 2010, Cuba announced the most sweeping and radical economic reforms the country has seen in decades. From ending state rationing to cutting one million public-sector jobs, one of the last communist bastions in the world has begun rolling back the state on an unprecedented scale.

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels from source to sea along the world's longest river, the Nile. His journey will take him from the holy source of the Blue Nile in the Ethiopian highlands, through the desert of Sudan and onwards through Egypt to the Mediterranean Sea.

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon follows the sacred waters of the Ganges from source to sea. Along the way he meets westerners in Rishikesh seeking spiritual enlightenment, and takes a holy dip in the fast-flowing waters at Haridwar.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie moors by Hampton Court Palace, but disaster strikes when a tree branch smashes a window. His dad joins to help through Hanwell locks.

programme.desc: Derek heads off to Colchester to pick up a 1960 Aston Martin DB4 that has been sitting under a tarpaulin in a garage that has collapsed round it for 40 years.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave is 'lost in France' en route to picking up a rare Honda NSX. Derek heads to a toy factory where some vintage motorbikes are stashed.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics

programme.title: Take The Ball. Pass The Ball
programme.desc: For four explosive years Pep Guardiola's Barcelona produced the greatest football in history, seducing fans around the world. In this exclusive, first-hand account of events between 2008 and 2012, the players themselves reveal the tension of the bitter Mourinho-Guardiola rivalry, the emotion of Abidal's fight against cancer to lift the European Cup and how Messi, the best footballer in the world, nearly didn't make it.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: When Thelma leaves Bob and goes back to her mother's, he makes the mistake of asking Terry's advice about how to win her back.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Thelma's still at home with her mother so it's up to Terry to soothe Bob's furrowed brow, but Bob is anxious that people will talk.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in County Durham for a visit to The Bowes Museum. Objects under scrutiny include a silver box given in thanks when troops liberated the Netherlands in World War Two, some of the most valuable chairs seen on the show, and a bust reputed to be cursed.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team of experts welcome thousands to Bletchley Park near Milton Keynes, home of the Enigma code-breaking team in World War Two.
